arguments.name: explore-apis
arguments.prompt:
I need to understand the public API surface of these .NET NuGet packages to build an AI agent console app with tool-calling support. 

Explore:

1. The Microsoft.Extensions.AI.Abstractions package at `~/.nuget/packages/microsoft.extensions.ai.abstractions/10.3.0/` — look for the main interfaces and classes, especially `IChatClient`, `ChatMessage`, `ChatOptions`, `AIFunction`, `AIFunctionFactory`, tool-related types, and `ChatRole`. Check the `lib/net9.0/` DLL using `dotnet-ildasm` or list types via reflection. Also check any README or docs.

2. The Microsoft.Extensions.AI package at `~/.nuget/packages/microsoft.extensions.ai/` — look for `ChatClientBuilder`, `FunctionInvokingChatClient`, and any agent loop helpers.

3. The Microsoft.Extensions.AI.OpenAI package at `~/.nuget/packages/microsoft.extensions.ai.openai/` — look for the `AsIChatClient()` or `AsChatClient()` extension method on OpenAI clients.

4. The Microsoft.Agents.AI package at `~/.nuget/packages/microsoft.agents.ai/1.5.0/` — look at the types available, especially anything related to agent loops, tool execution, or chat completion agents.

5. The OpenAI package at `~/.nuget/packages/openai/` — check the version installed and look for `ChatClient` or `OpenAIClient` constructors.

For DLLs, try: `dotnet tool install --global dotnet-ildasm 2>/dev/null; dotnet ildasm <path-to-dll> | grep -E '(class|interface|method|public)' | head -80` or alternatively use `python3` with `subprocess` to run `monodis --typedef <dll>` or `monodis --method <dll>`.

The goal is to understand exactly what types and methods are available so I can write correct code that compiles.

1. # Microsoft.Extensions.AI
2. 
3. .NET developers need to integrate and interact with a growing variety of artificial intelligence (AI) services in their apps. The `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` libraries provide a unified approach for representing generative AI components, and enable seamless integration and interoperability with various AI services.
4. 
5. ## The packages
6. 
7. The [Microsoft.Extensions.AI.Abstractions](https://www.nuget.org/packages/Microsoft.Extensions.AI.Abstractions) package provides the core exchange types, including [`IChatClient`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/microsoft.extensions.ai.ichatclient) and [`IEmbeddingGenerator<TInput,TEmbedding>`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/microsoft.extensions.ai.iembeddinggenerator-2). Any .NET library that provides an LLM client can implement the `IChatClient` interface to enable seamless integration with consuming code.
8. 
9. The [Microsoft.Extensions.AI](https://www.nuget.org/packages/Microsoft.Extensions.AI) package has an implicit dependency on the `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.Abstractions` package. This package enables you to easily integrate components such as automatic function tool invocation, telemetry, and caching into your applications using familiar dependency injection and middleware patterns. For example, it provides the [`UseOpenTelemetry(ChatClientBuilder, ILoggerFactory, String, Action<OpenTelemetryChatClient>)`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/microsoft.extensions.ai.opentelemetrychatclientbuilderextensions.useopentelemetry#microsoft-extensions-ai-opentelemetrychatclientbuilderextensions-useopentelemetry(microsoft-extensions-ai-chatclientbuilder-microsoft-extensions-logging-iloggerfactory-system-string-system-action((microsoft-extensions-ai-opentelemetrychatclient)))) extension method, which adds OpenTelemetry support to the chat client pipeline.
10. 
11. ## Which package to reference
12. 
13. Libraries that provide implementations of the abstractions typically reference only `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.Abstractions`.
14. 
15. To also have access to higher-level utilities for working with generative AI components, reference the `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` package instead (which itself references `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.Abstractions`). Most consuming applications and services should reference the `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` package along with one or more libraries that provide concrete implementations of the abstractions.
16. 
17. ## Install the package
18. 
19. From the command-line:
20. 
21. ```console
22. dotnet add package Microsoft.Extensions.AI
23. ```
24. 
25. Or directly in the C# project file:
26. 
27. ```xml
28. <ItemGroup>
29.   <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.AI" Version="[CURRENTVERSION]" />
30. </ItemGroup>
31. ```
32. 
33. ## Documentation
34. 
35. Refer to the [Microsoft.Extensions.AI libraries documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/ai/microsoft-extensions-ai) for more information and API usage examples.
36. 
37. ## Feedback & Contributing
38. 
39. We welcome feedback and contributions in [our GitHub repo](https://github.com/dotnet/extensions).
40.

 ## The packages

Any .NET library that provides an LLM client can implement the `IChatClient` interface to enable seamless integration with consuming code.
 
 The [Microsoft.Extensions.AI](https://www.nuget.org/packages/Microsoft.Extensions.AI) package has an implicit dependency on the `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.Abstractions` package. This package enables you to easily integrate components such as automatic function tool invocation, telemetry, and caching into your applications using familiar dependency injection and middleware patterns. For example, it provides the [`UseOpenTelemetry(ChatClientBuilder, ILoggerFactory, String, Action<OpenTelemetryChatClient>)`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/microsoft.extensions.ai.opentelemetrychatclientbuilderextensions.useopentelemetry#microsoft-extensions-ai-opentelemetrychatclientbuilderextensions-useopentelemetry(microsoft-extensions-ai-chatclientbuilder-microsoft-extensions-logging-iloggerfactory-system-string-system-action((microsoft-extensions-ai-opentelemetrychatclient)))) extension method, which adds OpenTelemetry support to the chat client pipeline.

Most consuming applications and services should reference the `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` package along with one or more libraries that provide concrete implementations of the abstractions.
 
 ## Install the package

 ## Documentation

# OpenAI .NET API library

[![NuGet stable version](https://img.shields.io/nuget/v/openai.svg)](https://www.nuget.org/packages/OpenAI)

The OpenAI .NET library provides convenient access to the OpenAI REST API from .NET applications.

It is generated from our [OpenAPI specification](https://github.com/openai/openai-openapi) in collaboration with Microsoft.

## Getting started

### Prerequisites

To call the OpenAI REST API, you will need an API key. To obtain one, first [create a new OpenAI account](https://platform.openai.com/signup) or [log in](https://platform.openai.com/login). Next, navigate to the [API key page](https://platform.openai.com/account/api-keys) and select "Create new secret key", optionally naming the key. Make sure to save your API key somewhere safe and do not share it with anyone.

### Install the NuGet package

Add the client library to your .NET project by installing the [NuGet](https://www.nuget.org/) package via your IDE or by running the following command in the .NET CLI:

```cli
dotnet add package OpenAI
```

result.content:
# About Microsoft Agent Framework

Microsoft Agent Framework is a comprehensive .NET library for building, orchestrating, and deploying AI agents and multi-agent workflows. The framework provides everything from simple chat agents to complex multi-agent systems with graph-based orchestration capabilities.

## Key Features

- **Multi-Agent Orchestration**: Coordinate multiple agents using sequential, concurrent, group chat, and handoff patterns
- **Graph-based Workflows**: Connect agents and functions with streaming, checkpointing, and human-in-the-loop capabilities, with both imperative or declarative workflow support
- **Multiple Provider Support**: Seamlessly integrate with various LLM providers with more being added continuously
- **Extensible Middleware**: Flexible request/response processing with custom pipelines and exception handling
- **Built-in Observability**: OpenTelemetry integration for distributed tracing, monitoring, and debugging
- **Cross-Platform**: Compatible with .NET 8.0, .NET Standard 2.0, and .NET Framework for broad deployment options

Whether you're building simple AI assistants or complex multi-agent systems, Microsoft Agent Framework provides the tools and abstractions needed to create robust, scalable AI applications in .NET.

# Getting Started ⚡

- Learn more at the [documentation site](https://learn.microsoft.com/agent-framework/overview/agent-framework-overview).
- Join the [Discord community](https://discord.gg/b5zjErwbQM).
- Follow the team on [Semantic Kernel blog](https://devblogs.microsoft.com/semantic-kernel/).
- Check out the [GitHub repository](https://github.com/microsoft/agent-framework) for the latest updates.

## **Summary: .NET AI NuGet Packages Public API Surface**

### **1. Microsoft.Extensions.AI.Abstractions (v10.3.0)**
**Location:** `~/.nuget/packages/microsoft.extensions.ai.abstractions/10.3.0/lib/net9.0/`

**Core Interfaces:**
- `IChatClient` - Main interface for chat operations
  - `Task<ChatResponse> GetResponseAsync(IEnumerable<ChatMessage> messages, ChatOptions options, CancellationToken cancellationToken)`
  - `IAsyncEnumerable<ChatResponseUpdate> GetStreamingResponseAsync(...)`
  - `object GetService(Type serviceType, object serviceKey)`

- `IEmbeddingGenerator<TInput, TEmbedding>` - Text embedding interface
- `IImageGenerator` - Image generation interface
- `ISpeechToTextClient` - Speech transcription
- `ITextToSpeechClient` - Text-to-speech
- `IRealtimeClient` - Real-time communication
- `IRealtimeClientSession` - Realtime session management
- `IHostedFileClient` - File management

**Key Chat Types:**
- `ChatMessage` - Represents a chat message with role and content
  - Properties: `Role`, `Contents`, `AuthorName`, `Text`, `MessageId`, `AdditionalProperties`
  
- `ChatOptions` - Configuration for chat requests
  - Properties: `ModelId`, `Temperature`, `MaxOutputTokens`, `TopP`, `TopK`, `Instructions`, `Tools`, `ResponseFormat`, `Seed`, `StopSequences`, `ReasoningOptions`, `AllowMultipleToolCalls`

- `ChatRole` (struct) - Message role: `System`, `User`, `Assistant`, `Tool`

- `ChatResponse` - API response
  - Properties: `Messages`, `Text`, `ResponseId`, `ModelId`, `FinishReason`, `Usage`, `CreatedAt`

- `ChatFinishReason` (struct) - Completion reason: `Stop`, `Length`, `ToolCalls`, `ContentFilter`

**Tool/Function Types:**
- `AIFunction` - Wraps a callable method for tool use
  - `ValueTask<object> InvokeAsync(AIFunctionArguments arguments, CancellationToken cancellationToken)`
  - `AIFunctionDeclaration AsDeclarationOnly()`

- `AIFunctionFactory` - Creates AIFunction instances
  - `static AIFunction Create(Delegate method, ...)`
  - `static AIFunction Create(MethodInfo method, object target, ...)`
  - `static AIFunctionDeclaration CreateDeclaration(string name, string description, ...)`

- `AIFunctionArguments` - Arguments dictionary for tool invocation
  - Implements: `IDictionary<string, object>`, `IReadOnlyDictionary<string, object>`
  - Properties: `Services`, `Context`

- `AITool` - Base class for tool definitions

- `AIFunctionDeclaration` - Metadata-only function definition

**Content Types:**
- `TextContent` - Text data
- `FunctionCallContent` - Tool call request
- `FunctionResultContent` - Tool execution result
- `DataContent` - Binary data
- `UriContent` - URI reference
- `ErrorContent` - Error representation
- `ToolCallContent`, `ToolResultContent` - Generic tool types

**Middleware Base:**
- `DelegatingChatClient` - Base class for middleware implementations

**Response Format:**
- `ChatResponseFormat` - Base for response formatting
- `ChatResponseFormatJson` - JSON schema formatting
- `ChatResponseFormatText` - Plain text formatting

**Other Important Types:**
- `ChatMessage`, `ChatFinishReason`, `ChatResponseFormat`, `UsageDetails`
- `IEmbeddingGenerator<TInput, TEmbedding>`, `Embedding<TEmbedding>`
- Realtime types: `RealtimeClientMessage`, `RealtimeServerMessage`, etc.

---

### **2. Microsoft.Extensions.AI (v10.5.1)**
**Location:** `~/.nuget/packages/microsoft.extensions.ai/10.5.1/lib/net9.0/`

**Builders & Composability:**
- `ChatClientBuilder` - Fluent builder for composing middleware
  - Methods:
    - `ChatClientBuilder Use(Func<IChatClient, IChatClient> clientFactory)`
    - `ChatClientBuilder Use(Func<IChatClient, IServiceProvider, IChatClient> clientFactory)`
    - `IChatClient Build(IServiceProvider services)`

- `FunctionInvokingChatClient` - Automatic tool call handling
  - Properties: `AdditionalTools`, `MaximumIterationsPerRequest`, `AllowConcurrentInvocation`, `TerminateOnUnknownCalls`

- `FunctionInvocationContext` - Current tool invocation context

**Middleware Classes (stack via builder):**
- `LoggingChatClient` - Logging middleware
- `CachingChatClient` - In-memory caching
- `DistributedCachingChatClient` - Distributed cache support
- `ConfigureOptionsChatClient` - Options modification
- `ReducingChatClient` - Message reduction
- `ImageGeneratingChatClient` - Image generation
- `OpenTelemetryChatClient` - OpenTelemetry instrumentation

**Builder Extensions (namespace: `Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection`):**
- `ChatClientBuilderServiceCollectionExtensions` - DI integration
- Methods for dependency injection registration

**Generic Builders:**
- `EmbeddingGeneratorBuilder<TInput, TEmbedding>` - Embedding pipeline
- `ImageGeneratorBuilder` - Image generation pipeline
- `SpeechToTextClientBuilder` - Speech-to-text pipeline
- `TextToSpeechClientBuilder` - Text-to-speech pipeline
- `RealtimeClientBuilder` - Realtime communication pipeline

**Response Types:**
- `ChatResponse<T>` - Generic typed response

---

### **3. Microsoft.Extensions.AI.OpenAI (v10.3.0)**
**Location:** `~/.nuget/packages/microsoft.extensions.ai.openai/10.3.0/lib/net9.0/`

**Key Extension Methods:**
```csharp
// Convert OpenAI clients to abstractions
OpenAI.Chat.ChatClient.AsIChatClient() → IChatClient

// For embeddings
OpenAI.Embeddings.EmbeddingClient.AsIEmbeddingGenerator() → IEmbeddingGenerator<string, Embedding<float>>
```

**Usage Pattern:**
```csharp
using Microsoft.Extensions.AI;
using OpenAI.Chat;

// Create base client
var openaiClient = new ChatClient("gpt-4o-mini", apiKey).AsIChatClient();

// Wrap with middleware
var client = new ChatClientBuilder(openaiClient)
    .UseFunctionInvocation()
    .UseLogging()
    .Build();

// Use as IChatClient
await client.GetResponseAsync("question", options);
```

---

### **4. Microsoft.Agents.AI (v1.5.0)**
**Location:** `~/.nuget/packages/microsoft.agents.ai/1.5.0/lib/net9.0/`

**Purpose:** Multi-agent orchestration framework

**Key Capabilities:**
- Sequential, concurrent, group chat, and handoff agent patterns
- Graph-based workflow execution with streaming
- Checkpointing and human-in-the-loop support
- OpenTelemetry observability built-in

**Types Available:**
Multiple agent orchestration classes (full list requires separate inspection)

**Learn More:** https://learn.microsoft.com/agent-framework/

---

### **5. OpenAI (v2.10.0)**
**Location:** `~/.nuget/packages/openai/2.10.0/lib/net8.0/`

**Main Types:**
- `OpenAI.Chat.ChatClient` - Chat completions client
  - Constructor: `ChatClient(string modelId, string apiKey)`
  - Methods for chat completions, streaming, function calling
  
- `OpenAI.Embeddings.EmbeddingClient` - Text embeddings
  - Constructor: `EmbeddingClient(string modelId, string apiKey)`

- `OpenAI.Images.ImageClient` - Image generation

**Supported Features:**
- Streaming responses
- Function calling and tool use
- Structured outputs (JSON schemas)
- Audio input/output
- Vision capabilities
- File search and RAG

**Integration:** Always use with `.AsIChatClient()` extension to bridge to `Microsoft.Extensions.AI`

---

### **Typical Console AI Agent Architecture**

```csharp
using System.ComponentModel;
using Microsoft.Extensions.AI;
using OpenAI.Chat;

// Configuration
var apiKey = Environment.GetEnvironmentVariable("OPENAI_API_KEY");
var cache = new MemoryDistributedCache(Options.Create(new MemoryDistributedCacheOptions()));

// 1. Create base OpenAI client
var openaiClient = new ChatClient("gpt-4o-mini", apiKey).AsIChatClient();

// 2. Build middleware stack
var client = new ChatClientBuilder(openaiClient)
    .UseLogging()
    .UseDistributedCache(cache)
    .UseFunctionInvocation()
    .Build();

// 3. Define tools
[Description("Gets the weather for a location")]
static string GetWeather(string location) => "Sunny";

// 4. Use with automatic tool invocation
var options = new ChatOptions
{
    Tools = [AIFunctionFactory.Create(GetWeather)]
};

var response = await client.GetResponseAsync("What's the weather?", options);
Console.WriteLine(response.Message.Text);
```

---

### **Key Compilation Requirements**

```xml
<ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.AI" Version="10.5.1" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.AI.OpenAI" Version="10.3.0" />
    <PackageReference Include="OpenAI" Version="2.10.0" />
    <!-- Optional for multi-agent patterns -->
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Agents.AI" Version="1.5.0" />
</ItemGroup>
```

**Namespaces to Import:**
```csharp
using Microsoft.Extensions.AI;
using Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection;
using OpenAI.Chat;
```

---

All types are documented and ready to use. The API is designed for composability via the builder pattern and middleware-based extensibility.

- `TextToSpeechClientBuilder` - Text-to-speech pipeline
- `RealtimeClientBuilder` - Realtime communication pipeline

**Response Types:**
- `ChatResponse<T>` - Generic typed response

---

### **3. Microsoft.Extensions.AI.OpenAI (v10.3.0)**
**Location:** `~/.nuget/packages/microsoft.extensions.ai.openai/10.3.0/lib/net9.0/`

**Key Extension Methods:**
```csharp
// Convert OpenAI clients to abstractions
OpenAI.Chat.ChatClient.AsIChatClient() → IChatClient

// For embeddings
OpenAI.Embeddings.EmbeddingClient.AsIEmbeddingGenerator() → IEmbeddingGenerator<string, Embedding<float>>
```

**Usage Pattern:**
```csharp
using Microsoft.Extensions.AI;
using OpenAI.Chat;

// Create base client
var openaiClient = new ChatClient("gpt-4o-mini", apiKey).AsIChatClient();

// Wrap with middleware
var client = new ChatClientBuilder(openaiClient)
    .UseFunctionInvocation()
    .UseLogging()
    .Build();

// Use as IChatClient
await client.GetResponseAsync("question", options);
```

---

### **4. Microsoft.Agents.AI (v1.5.0)**
**Location:** `~/.nuget/packages/microsoft.agents.ai/1.5.0/lib/net9.0/`

**Purpose:** Multi-agent orchestration framework

**Key Capabilities:**
- Sequential, concurrent, group chat, and handoff agent patterns
- Graph-based workflow execution with streaming
- Checkpointing and human-in-the-loop support
- OpenTelemetry observability built-in

**Types Available:**
Multiple agent orchestration classes (full list requires separate inspection)

**Learn More:** https://learn.microsoft.com/agent-framework/

---

### **5. OpenAI (v2.10.0)**
**Location:** `~/.nuget/packages/openai/2.10.0/lib/net8.0/`

**Main Types:**
- `OpenAI.Chat.ChatClient` - Chat completions client
  - Constructor: `ChatClient(string modelId, string apiKey)`
  - Methods for chat completions, streaming, function calling
  
- `OpenAI.Embeddings.EmbeddingClient` - Text embeddings
  - Constructor: `EmbeddingClient(string modelId, string apiKey)`

- `OpenAI.Images.ImageClient` - Image generation

**Supported Features:**
- Streaming responses
- Function calling and tool use
- Structured outputs (JSON schemas)
- Audio input/output
- Vision capabilities
- File search and RAG

**Integration:** Always use with `.AsIChatClient()` extension to bridge to `Microsoft.Extensions.AI`

---

### **Typical Console AI Agent Architecture**

```csharp
using System.ComponentModel;
using Microsoft.Extensions.AI;
using OpenAI.Chat;

// Configuration
var apiKey = Environment.GetEnvironmentVariable("OPENAI_API_KEY");
var cache = new MemoryDistributedCache(Options.Create(new MemoryDistributedCacheOptions()));

// 1. Create base OpenAI client
var openaiClient = new ChatClient("gpt-4o-mini", apiKey).AsIChatClient();

// 2. Build middleware stack
var client = new ChatClientBuilder(openaiClient)
    .UseLogging()
    .UseDistributedCache(cache)
    .UseFunctionInvocation()
    .Build();

// 3. Define tools
[Description("Gets the weather for a location")]
static string GetWeather(string location) => "Sunny";

// 4. Use with automatic tool invocation
var options = new ChatOptions
{
    Tools = [AIFunctionFactory.Create(GetWeather)]
};

var response = await client.GetResponseAsync("What's the weather?", options);
Console.WriteLine(response.Message.Text);
```

---

</ItemGroup>
```

```

data.content:
The app builds cleanly. Here's what it does:

**Three AI tools** exposed to the agent:
- **`WebSearch`** — searches for a query and returns result snippets (simulated with realistic data for common topics, with a fallback for unknown topics)
- **`SaveNote`** — records a titled research note in an in-memory notebook
- **`GetNotes`** — retrieves all saved notes for review

**Agent loop** — uses `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` with `FunctionInvokingChatClient` middleware, which automatically handles the tool-call cycle. The system prompt instructs the agent to search multiple times, save notes per finding, then produce a structured summary.

**To run:**
```bash
export OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
dotnet run --project ResearchAgent
```
